content:
Hi, I currently working in this issue: https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/issues/1119
and I don't understand the flow to find the branches and mantainers. Thanks in advance for help.
If you haven't seen it, then please see https://rust-for-linux.com/contributing. If you have seen it, and something is unclear, please let us know.
For me is unclear which branch I should send and how. For example, I imagine that most suitable tree is staging/dev, but I can't find it. I only can find the staging for example.
I mean, I should use the upstream repo or the github rust for linux repo?

In a nutshell, you should use the tree that makes the most sense for the change you are doing. For instance, you can use rust-next if you need recent changes that are there (e.g. something changed that would affect your patch), but you could otherwise also use e.g. the latest mainline tag otherwise.

staging/dev integrates several branches and thus it may have a lot of patches that are not upstream yet, so e.g. for an upstream cleanup or a small feature that does not depend on things in staging/dev it does not make much sense.

but I can't find it. I only can find the staging for example.

I am not sure what you mean -- the branch is in our repository (though I don't think you need it).

Miguel Ojeda said:

but I can't find it. I only can find the staging for example.

I am not sure what you mean -- the branch is in our repository (though I don't think you need it).

I see, thanks for the answers. I mean, for example with I wanna send something from staging i just get the git://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/gregkh/staging.git , in R4L i can't find the git for staging/dev.

Every2 said:

I see, thanks for the answers. I mean, for example with I wanna send something from staging i just get the git://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/gregkh/staging.git , in R4L i can't find the git for staging/dev.

You're welcome! I think you perhaps were confusing the staging subsystem with our staging branches. Please see https://rust-for-linux.com/branches#topic-branches:

Some of these branches may contain work-in-progress code (similar to staging trees) that may not be suitable for upstreaming or general usage yet. Please check the details of each branch.

Miguel Ojeda said:

Every2 said:

I see, thanks for the answers. I mean, for example with I wanna send something from staging i just get the git://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/gregkh/staging.git , in R4L i can't find the git for staging/dev.

You're welcome! I think you perhaps were confusing the staging subsystem with our staging branches. Please see https://rust-for-linux.com/branches#topic-branches:

Some of these branches may contain work-in-progress code (similar to staging trees) that may not be suitable for upstreaming or general usage yet. Please check the details of each branch.

Yeah, I was a bit lost. I didn't knew that tree is in github. I'm more confortable with linux system. Sorry about the mistakes. Thanks @Miguel Ojeda and @Alice Ryhl !
Every2 has marked this topic as resolved.
